subtitle: Fool's Gold With Real Power to Manifest Wealth
heroDesc: Pyrite gleams with the brassy golden luster that earned it the nickname 'Fool's Gold,' but anyone who works with this stone knows its power is anything but foolish. Its perfect cubic crystals and striated faces look engineered by nature itself, embodying the marriage of raw elemental force and geometric precision. Pyrite is the crystal you call upon when you are ready to stop dreaming about abundance and start building it, one confident action at a time.

perspectives.scientific: Pyrite is an iron disulfide mineral (FeS2) with a Mohs hardness of 6 to 6.5 and a distinctive pale brass-yellow coloration that historically led prospectors to mistake it for gold. It crystallizes most commonly in the isometric (cubic) system, often forming perfect cubes and pyritohedra with striated faces that reflect its internal atomic structure. Major deposits occur in Spain (Navajun, famous for perfect cubic crystals), Peru, Italy, China, and the United States. Pyrite forms in sedimentary, metamorphic, and igneous environments and is one of the most abundant sulfide minerals on Earth. Important safety note: pyrite contains sulfur and should never be used in direct-contact gemstone elixirs or ingested.
perspectives.spiritual: Pyrite is a formidable Solar Plexus Chakra stone, radiating the frequency of personal power, assertive confidence, and unwavering will. Associated with the zodiac sign of Leo and resonating with the Fire and Air elements, pyrite carries the energy of the midday sun at its peak, illuminating and empowering. In metaphysical traditions, it is revered as a stone of wealth manifestation, used to attract financial abundance, defend against negative energy, and stimulate the intellect in service of material success. Its metallic golden sheen has made it a symbol of hidden value and the transformative power of perception.
perspectives.psychological: The metallic gold appearance of pyrite triggers associations with achievement, value, and success, activating the brain's reward centers and reinforcing a success-oriented mindset. In therapeutic settings, pyrite serves as a tactile anchor for confidence-building exercises, its weight and cool metallic surface grounding the user during moments of anxiety or self-doubt. The stone's geometric perfection appeals to the human desire for order and structure, making it a powerful symbol for those seeking to bring disciplined focus to their ambitions.

attributes.origin: Spain (Navajun), Peru, Italy, China, USA, Germany, Russia

benefits.title: Wealth & Abundance Manifestation
benefits.desc: Pyrite is one of the most powerful crystals for attracting financial prosperity, business success, and material abundance. Its golden energy resonates with the frequency of wealth and is a staple in money altars, prosperity grids, and wealth-attracting rituals across cultures.

benefits.title: Unshakable Confidence & Willpower
benefits.desc: Pyrite activates the Solar Plexus Chakra with an assertive, commanding energy that dissolves self-doubt and replaces it with unshakable inner conviction. When you need to negotiate, lead, or stand your ground, pyrite provides the energetic backbone to do so without flinching.

benefits.title: Intellectual Sharpness & Memory
benefits.desc: Pyrite stimulates the intellect, improves focus, and enhances recall, making it an excellent study companion for demanding academic or professional work. Its Air element connection supports clear analytical thinking and strategic planning.

benefits.title: Energetic Shielding & Protection
benefits.desc: Pyrite creates a defensive shield around the aura that deflects negative energy, environmental pollutants, and psychic manipulation. It is particularly effective in workplace environments where office politics, gossip, or competitive hostility drain your energy.

benefits.title: Action & Productivity
benefits.desc: Where some crystals encourage dreaming, pyrite demands doing. Its high-frequency Fire energy combats procrastination and lethargy, pushing you to take decisive, productive action toward your goals every single day.

benefits.title: Leadership & Assertiveness
benefits.desc: Pyrite emboldens you to speak your truth, set clear boundaries, and step into leadership roles with natural authority. It supports the kind of confident decision-making that inspires trust and commands respect from others.

benefits.title: Physical Vitality & Stamina
benefits.desc: In crystal healing traditions, pyrite is believed to strengthen the circulatory and respiratory systems, boost oxygenation of the blood, and combat chronic fatigue. Its energizing presence is especially supportive during physically demanding periods of life.

usageScenes.title: Wear It Daily
usageScenes.desc: Wear pyrite as a bracelet or ring when you need an extra layer of confidence and protection, particularly during business meetings, negotiations, or any situation requiring assertiveness. Its grounding weight and golden gleam serve as constant reminders of your innate power.

usageScenes.title: Meditation Companion
usageScenes.desc: Hold pyrite over your Solar Plexus (upper abdomen) during meditation to charge your personal power center and visualize golden light filling your core with strength and purpose. Use it before challenging situations to fortify your energy field.

usageScenes.title: Home Sanctuary
usageScenes.desc: Place pyrite clusters or cubes on your desk, near your cash register, or in the wealth corner of your office to attract financial opportunities and shield against workplace negativity. A pyrite sun on your windowsill catches and amplifies golden light throughout the day.

usageScenes.title: Ritual & Ceremony
usageScenes.desc: Include pyrite in abundance grids alongside citrine and green aventurine to create a triple-force prosperity altar. Use it in candle magic focused on career advancement or financial breakthroughs, placing the stone at the base of a gold or yellow candle.

pairings.name: Citrine
pairings.why: Citrine and pyrite form the ultimate wealth-attracting pair, with citrine bringing joyful abundance energy and pyrite adding structured, disciplined manifestation power. Together they create a magnetic field that draws prosperity from multiple directions.

pairings.name: Green Aventurine
pairings.why: Green aventurine provides the lucky, opportunistic energy while pyrite supplies the assertive follow-through, together creating a complete wealth attraction system from chance encounter to sealed deal.

pairings.name: Tiger Eye
pairings.why: Tiger Eye complements pyrite's bold confidence with grounded discernment and strategic patience, preventing impulsive decisions while maintaining the drive to succeed. This pairing balances aggressive ambition with wise, calculated action.

faqs.title: Is pyrite safe to wear?
faqs.content: Yes, pyrite is perfectly safe to wear as jewelry. While it contains sulfur and iron, these elements are locked within the crystal structure and do not release through skin contact. However, pyrite should never be used in direct-contact gemstone elixirs (where the stone touches water you intend to drink), as sulfur compounds can leach into the water and cause gastrointestinal distress.

faqs.title: Why is pyrite called Fool's Gold?
faqs.content: During the gold rush era, inexperienced prospectors frequently mistook pyrite's brass-yellow metallic luster for actual gold, leading to disappointing discoveries at the assay office. The nickname stuck as a reminder that appearances can be deceiving. Ironically, modern crystal practitioners consider pyrite's 'foolish' gold to hold genuine energetic value for manifesting real wealth.

faqs.title: How do I care for pyrite?
faqs.content: Keep pyrite dry and store it away from humidity, as prolonged moisture exposure can cause oxidation that leads to a dull surface or, in extreme cases, pyrite decay (formation of sulfuric acid). Wipe it with a soft dry cloth after handling, and avoid storing it in sealed plastic bags where moisture can accumulate. Keep it away from salt water and chemical cleaners.

faqs.title: Can pyrite go in water?
faqs.content: No, pyrite should not be immersed in water. When exposed to moisture and oxygen over time, pyrite can oxidize and produce sulfuric acid, leading to a deteriorating process known as pyrite decay. Do not use pyrite in water-based cleansing rituals, gemstone elixirs, or salt water baths. Cleanse it instead with sage smoke, sound, or by placing it on a selenite plate.

faqs.title: What is a pyrite sun?
faqs.content: A pyrite sun (also called a pyrite dollar or mineral sun) is a flat, disc-shaped pyrite formation that radiates outward in a sunburst pattern, found primarily in coal mines near Sparta, Illinois. These natural formations occur when pyrite crystallizes in thin, flat layers between shale or coal seams, and they are highly prized by collectors and crystal practitioners as symbols of solar energy and radiant power.
